methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Qilimanjaro, a Series A quantum computing company founded by Pol Forn-Díaz, is evaluated against a sector universe of 11 peers: three public companies (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Systems) and eight private firms including Quantinuum and Infleqtion. The company has raised $10M to date.

Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier to the $10M capital raised, yielding a $20M implied valuation. This reflects typical step-up expectations between Series A and subsequent funding rounds. Peer-median triangulation does not apply; while seven quantum computing peers exist in the dataset, insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables with matching valuation tiers preclude reliable extraction of a median multiple. Constructing a broad sector median would introduce unacceptable heterogeneity across vastly different company maturity levels and capital structures.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $17M–$23M, with a central estimate of $20M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method; the primary uncertainty is the appropriateness of the 2.0x progression multiple for quantum computing specifically, a sector characterized by high technical risk and capital intensity. Validation would require additional comparable transactions or disclosure of Qilimanjaro's specific metrics (revenue, burn rate, technical milestones, IP position) relative to peer benchmarks. The undisclosed current valuation limits post-hoc calibration of the model.
